title: nymphaea × intermedia
intro: Nymphaea × intermedia, also known as the water lily or Victoria amazonica, is a hybrid aquatic plant belonging to the Nymphaea genus. Traditionally, the roots of this plant have been used in various herbal preparations due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as the ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The health benefits of Nymphaea × intermedia include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce anxiety, and improve sleep quality, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the roots of this plant are often used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
